title: API Engineering & REST Systems
subtitle: Design APIs that age well — versioning, auth, errors, performance
description: A practical course on designing and operating HTTP APIs that survive contact with real consumers. Covers REST principles, resource modeling, versioning, authentication, error semantics, pagination, rate limiting, caching, observability, and the trade-offs vs gRPC and GraphQL. Optimized for engineers building APIs that other people will depend on.
audience: Backend engineers, full-stack devs, integration engineers. Comfortable with HTTP basics; have built at least one API endpoint before.
prerequisites: Understand HTTP basics (methods, status codes, headers)
Built at least one API endpoint
outcomes: Design clean REST resources that match consumer needs
Choose HTTP methods and status codes correctly
Authenticate with API keys, OAuth, JWTs at the right time
Design error responses that are debuggable
Document with OpenAPI; generate clients automatically
Operate APIs: rate limits, observability, deprecation

modules.title: Design fundamentals
modules.objective: Get the shape right before the code.

lessons.title: REST principles — what they really mean
HTTP methods and what they mean
Status codes: the conversation with the client

modules.title: Authentication and authorization
modules.objective: Lock things down without making consumers cry.

lessons.title: API keys, basic auth, bearer tokens
OAuth 2.1 and the authorization code flow
JWTs: when they help, when they hurt

modules.title: Working with collections
modules.objective: Pagination, filtering, sorting, errors that scale.

modules.title: Operating real APIs
modules.objective: Make APIs that don't fall over.

lessons.title: REST vs gRPC vs GraphQL — picking right
